templates.name: Grafana
templates.compose:
services:
  grafana:
    image: grafana/grafana:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:3000"
    environment:
      - GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_USER={{ADMIN_USER}}
      - GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}
      - GF_USERS_ALLOW_SIGN_UP=false
    volumes:
      - grafana_data:/var/lib/grafana
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  grafana_data:
templates.securityNotes: Use strong admin password
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Log in with admin credentials
2. Add data sources (Prometheus, InfluxDB, etc.)
3. Create dashboards

templates.name: Prometheus
templates.compose:
services:
  prometheus:
    image: prom/prometheus:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:9090"
    command:
      - '--config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml'
      - '--storage.tsdb.path=/prometheus'
      - '--web.enable-lifecycle'
    volumes:
      - prometheus_data:/prometheus
      - prometheus_config:/etc/prometheus
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  prometheus_data:
  prometheus_config:
templates.securityNotes: No authentication by default. Use reverse proxy with auth.
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Create prometheus.yml configuration
2. Add scrape targets
3. Configure alerting rules

templates.name: Uptime Kuma
templates.description: Self-hosted monitoring tool like Uptime Robot
templates.compose:
services:
  uptime-kuma:
    image: louislam/uptime-kuma:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:3001"
    volumes:
      - uptime_kuma_data:/app/data
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  uptime_kuma_data:
templates.securityNotes: Create admin account on first access
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Create admin account
2. Add monitors for your services
3. Configure notifications

templates.name: Netdata
templates.compose:
services:
  netdata:
    image: netdata/netdata:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:19999"
    cap_add:
      - SYS_PTRACE
    security_opt:
      - apparmor:unconfined
    volumes:
      - netdata_config:/etc/netdata
      - netdata_lib:/var/lib/netdata
      - netdata_cache:/var/cache/netdata
      - /etc/passwd:/host/etc/passwd:ro
      - /etc/group:/host/etc/group:ro
      - /proc:/host/proc:ro
      - /sys:/host/sys:ro
      - /etc/os-release:/host/etc/os-release:ro
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  netdata_config:
  netdata_lib:
  netdata_cache:
templates.securityNotes: Netdata has full host visibility. Restrict access to trusted users.
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Access dashboard
2. Configure alerts
3. Set up cloud sync (optional)

templates.name: GlitchTip
templates.compose:
services:
  glitchtip:
    image: glitchtip/glitchtip:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:8080"
    environment:
      - DATABASE_URL=postgres://glitchtip:{{DB_PASSWORD}}@db:5432/glitchtip
      - SECRET_KEY={{SECRET_KEY}}
      - GLITCHTIP_DOMAIN={{DOMAIN}}
      - DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL=noreply@example.com
      - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - db
      - redis

  worker:
    image: glitchtip/glitchtip:latest
    command: ./bin/run-celery-with-beat.sh
    environment:
      - DATABASE_URL=postgres://glitchtip:{{DB_PASSWORD}}@db:5432/glitchtip
      - SECRET_KEY={{SECRET_KEY}}
      - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - db
      - redis

  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER=glitchtip
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{DB_PASSWORD}}
      - POSTGRES_DB=glitchtip
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    restart: unless-stopped

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  postgres_data:

variables.name: DOMAIN
variables.description: Domain (must start with http:// or https://)

templates.securityNotes: Create superuser after deployment
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Run migrations
2. Create superuser
3. Create organization and project

templates.name: Mailu
templates.compose:
services:
  front:
    image: ghcr.io/mailu/nginx:2024.06
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:80"
      - "{{HTTPS_PORT}}:443"
      - "{{SMTP_PORT}}:25"
      - "{{IMAP_PORT}}:143"
      - "{{SUBMISSION_PORT}}:587"
    environment:
      - TZ={{TIMEZONE}}
    volumes:
      - mailu_certs:/certs
      - mailu_overrides:/overrides
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - admin
      - imap
      - smtp

  admin:
    image: ghcr.io/mailu/admin:2024.06
    environment:
      - SECRET_KEY={{SECRET_KEY}}
      - DOMAIN={{DOMAIN}}
      - HOSTNAMES={{HOSTNAMES}}
      - TZ={{TIMEZONE}}
    volumes:
      - mailu_data:/data
      - mailu_dkim:/dkim
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - redis

  imap:
    image: ghcr.io/mailu/dovecot:2024.06
    environment:
      - TZ={{TIMEZONE}}
    volumes:
      - mailu_mail:/mail
      - mailu_overrides:/overrides
    restart: unless-stopped

  smtp:
    image: ghcr.io/mailu/postfix:2024.06
    environment:
      - TZ={{TIMEZONE}}
    volumes:
      - mailu_overrides:/overrides
    restart: unless-stopped

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    volumes:
      - mailu_redis:/data
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  mailu_certs:
  mailu_overrides:
  mailu_data:
  mailu_dkim:
  mailu_mail:
  mailu_redis:
templates.securityNotes: Requires DNS configuration (MX, SPF, DKIM, DMARC records)
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Configure DNS records
2. Create admin account
3. Add mail domains

templates.name: Postal
templates.description: Mail delivery platform for sending transactional emails
templates.compose:
services:
  postal:
    image: ghcr.io/postalserver/postal:3.3.4
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:5000"
      - "{{SMTP_PORT}}:25"
    environment:
      - POSTAL_FNAME=Postal
      - POSTAL_LNAME=Admin
      - POSTAL_EMAIL=admin@example.com
      - MAIN_DB_HOST=mysql
      - MAIN_DB_USERNAME=root
      - MAIN_DB_PASSWORD={{DB_ROOT_PASSWORD}}
      - MESSAGE_DB_HOST=mysql
      - MESSAGE_DB_USERNAME=root
      - MESSAGE_DB_PASSWORD={{DB_ROOT_PASSWORD}}
      - RABBITMQ_HOST=rabbitmq
    volumes:
      - postal_config:/config
      - postal_storage:/storage
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - mysql
      - rabbitmq

  mysql:
    image: mariadb:10.11
    environment:
      - MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD={{DB_ROOT_PASSWORD}}
      - MYSQL_DATABASE=postal
    volumes:
      - mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql
    restart: unless-stopped

  rabbitmq:
    image: rabbitmq:3-management
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  postal_config:
  postal_storage:
  mysql_data:
templates.securityNotes: Requires initial setup with postal initialize command
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Run postal initialize
2. Create admin user
3. Configure DNS records

templates.name: Nextcloud
templates.description: Self-hosted file sync and collaboration platform
templates.compose:
services:
  nextcloud:
    image: nextcloud:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:80"
    environment:
      - MYSQL_HOST=db
      - MYSQL_DATABASE=nextcloud
      - MYSQL_USER=nextcloud
      - MYSQL_PASSWORD={{DB_PASSWORD}}
      - NEXTCLOUD_ADMIN_USER={{ADMIN_USER}}
      - NEXTCLOUD_ADMIN_PASSWORD={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}
      - NEXTCLOUD_TRUSTED_DOMAINS={{TRUSTED_DOMAINS}}
    volumes:
      - nextcloud_data:/var/www/html
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy

  db:
    image: mysql:8.0
    environment:
      - MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD={{DB_ROOT_PASSWORD}}
      - MYSQL_DATABASE=nextcloud
      - MYSQL_USER=nextcloud
      - MYSQL_PASSWORD={{DB_PASSWORD}}
    volumes:
      - mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "mysqladmin ping -h localhost -u root -p$$MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 30s

volumes:
  nextcloud_data:
  mysql_data:
templates.securityNotes: Add your domain to trusted domains. Use HTTPS in production.
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Log in with admin credentials
2. Install recommended apps
3. Configure external storage

templates.name: MinIO
templates.compose:
services:
  minio:
    image: minio/minio:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:9000"
      - "{{CONSOLE_PORT}}:9001"
    environment:
      - MINIO_ROOT_USER={{ROOT_USER}}
      - MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD={{ROOT_PASSWORD}}
    command: server /data --console-address ":9001"
    volumes:
      - minio_data:/data
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  minio_data:
templates.securityNotes: Use strong root password (8+ chars). Use HTTPS in production.
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Access console
2. Create buckets
3. Configure access policies

templates.name: Immich
templates.description: Self-hosted photo and video backup (Google Photos alternative)
templates.compose:
services:
  immich-server:
    image: ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-server:release
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:2283"
    environment:
      - DB_HOSTNAME=db
      - DB_USERNAME=immich
      - DB_PASSWORD={{DB_PASSWORD}}
      - DB_DATABASE_NAME=immich
      - REDIS_HOSTNAME=redis
    volumes:
      - immich_upload:/usr/src/app/upload
      - /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - db
      - redis

  immich-machine-learning:
    image: ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-machine-learning:release
    volumes:
      - immich_model_cache:/cache
    restart: unless-stopped

  db:
    image: tensorchord/pgvecto-rs:pg16-v0.2.1
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER=immich
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{DB_PASSWORD}}
      - POSTGRES_DB=immich
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    restart: unless-stopped

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  immich_upload:
  immich_model_cache:
  postgres_data:
templates.securityNotes: Requires 4GB+ RAM for ML features
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Create admin account
2. Install mobile app
3. Configure backup settings

templates.name: PhotoPrism
templates.compose:
services:
  photoprism:
    image: photoprism/photoprism:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:2342"
    environment:
      - PHOTOPRISM_ADMIN_USER={{ADMIN_USER}}
      - PHOTOPRISM_ADMIN_PASSWORD={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}
      - PHOTOPRISM_SITE_URL={{SITE_URL}}
      - PHOTOPRISM_DATABASE_DRIVER=mysql
      - PHOTOPRISM_DATABASE_SERVER=db:3306
      - PHOTOPRISM_DATABASE_NAME=photoprism
      - PHOTOPRISM_DATABASE_USER=photoprism
      - PHOTOPRISM_DATABASE_PASSWORD={{DB_PASSWORD}}
    volumes:
      - photoprism_originals:/photoprism/originals
      - photoprism_storage:/photoprism/storage
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - db

  db:
    image: mariadb:10.11
    environment:
      - MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD={{DB_ROOT_PASSWORD}}
      - MARIADB_DATABASE=photoprism
      - MARIADB_USER=photoprism
      - MARIADB_PASSWORD={{DB_PASSWORD}}
    volumes:
      - mariadb_data:/var/lib/mysql
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  photoprism_originals:
  photoprism_storage:
  mariadb_data:
templates.securityNotes: Initial indexing can take hours for large libraries
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Log in with admin credentials
2. Upload or mount photos
3. Run initial indexing

templates.name: Syncthing
templates.compose:
services:
  syncthing:
    image: lscr.io/linuxserver/syncthing:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:8384"
      - "22000:22000/tcp"
      - "22000:22000/udp"
      - "21027:21027/udp"
    environment:
      - PUID=1000
      - PGID=1000
    volumes:
      - syncthing_config:/config
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  syncthing_config:
templates.securityNotes: Set up GUI password after first access
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Access web UI
2. Set up GUI password
3. Add devices and folders to sync

templates.name: Paperless-ngx
templates.compose:
services:
  paperless:
    image: ghcr.io/paperless-ngx/paperless-ngx:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:8000"
    environment:
      - PAPERLESS_REDIS=redis://redis:6379
      - PAPERLESS_DBHOST=db
      - PAPERLESS_DBNAME=paperless
      - PAPERLESS_DBUSER=paperless
      - PAPERLESS_DBPASS={{DB_PASSWORD}}
      - PAPERLESS_SECRET_KEY={{SECRET_KEY}}
      - PAPERLESS_URL={{PAPERLESS_URL}}
    volumes:
      - paperless_data:/usr/src/paperless/data
      - paperless_media:/usr/src/paperless/media
      - paperless_consume:/usr/src/paperless/consume
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - db
      - redis

  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER=paperless
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{DB_PASSWORD}}
      - POSTGRES_DB=paperless
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    restart: unless-stopped

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  paperless_data:
  paperless_media:
  paperless_consume:
  postgres_data:
templates.securityNotes: Create superuser: docker exec paperless python manage.py createsuperuser
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Create superuser
2. Upload documents
3. Configure consumption folder

templates.name: Loki
templates.compose:
services:
  loki:
    image: grafana/loki:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:3100"
    command: -config.file=/etc/loki/local-config.yaml
    volumes:
      - loki_data:/loki
    restart: unless-stopped

  promtail:
    image: grafana/promtail:latest
    volumes:
      - /var/log:/var/log:ro
      - promtail_config:/etc/promtail
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - loki

volumes:
  loki_data:
  promtail_config:
templates.securityNotes: No authentication by default. Use reverse proxy with auth.
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Configure Promtail
2. Add Loki as Grafana data source
3. Create log dashboards

templates.name: SigNoz
templates.compose:
services:
  signoz:
    image: signoz/signoz:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:3301"
    environment:
      - SIGNOZ_WEB_URL={{WEB_URL}}
    volumes:
      - signoz_data:/var/lib/signoz
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - clickhouse

  clickhouse:
    image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:latest
    volumes:
      - clickhouse_data:/var/lib/clickhouse
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  signoz_data:
  clickhouse_data:
templates.securityNotes: SigNoz requires significant resources
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Create admin account
2. Configure data sources
3. Set up instrumentation

templates.name: Mailcow
templates.compose:
services:
  nginx:
    image: ghcr.io/mailcow/nginx:1.03
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:443"
      - "{{HTTP_PORT}}:80"
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - php-fpm

  php-fpm:
    image: ghcr.io/mailcow/phpfpm:1.92
    environment:
      - DBNAME=mailcow
      - DBUSER=mailcow
      - DBPASS={{DB_PASSWORD}}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - mysql
      - redis

  mysql:
    image: mariadb:11
    environment:
      - MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD={{DB_ROOT_PASSWORD}}
      - MARIADB_DATABASE=mailcow
      - MARIADB_USER=mailcow
      - MARIADB_PASSWORD={{DB_PASSWORD}}
    volumes:
      - mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql
    restart: unless-stopped

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped

  dovecot:
    image: ghcr.io/mailcow/dovecot:1.30
    restart: unless-stopped

  postfix:
    image: ghcr.io/mailcow/postfix:1.77
    ports:
      - "25:25"
      - "465:465"
      - "587:587"
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  mysql_data:
templates.securityNotes: Configure DNS records (MX, SPF, DKIM, DMARC)
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Configure DNS records
2. Add domains
3. Create mailboxes

templates.name: Filestash
templates.description: Web-based file manager with S3, SFTP, FTP support
templates.compose:
services:
  filestash:
    image: machines/filestash:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:8334"
    volumes:
      - filestash_data:/app/data/state
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  filestash_data:
templates.securityNotes: Configure backends and authentication in admin
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Access admin interface
2. Configure backends (S3, SFTP, etc.)
3. Set up authentication

templates.name: ownCloud
templates.compose:
services:
  owncloud:
    image: owncloud/server:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:8080"
    environment:
      - OWNCLOUD_DOMAIN={{DOMAIN}}
      - OWNCLOUD_DB_TYPE=mysql
      - OWNCLOUD_DB_HOST=db
      - OWNCLOUD_DB_NAME=owncloud
      - OWNCLOUD_DB_USERNAME=owncloud
      - OWNCLOUD_DB_PASSWORD={{DB_PASSWORD}}
      - OWNCLOUD_ADMIN_USERNAME={{ADMIN_USER}}
      - OWNCLOUD_ADMIN_PASSWORD={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}
      - OWNCLOUD_REDIS_ENABLED=true
      - OWNCLOUD_REDIS_HOST=redis
    volumes:
      - owncloud_data:/mnt/data
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - db
      - redis

  db:
    image: mariadb:11
    environment:
      - MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD={{DB_ROOT_PASSWORD}}
      - MARIADB_DATABASE=owncloud
      - MARIADB_USER=owncloud
      - MARIADB_PASSWORD={{DB_PASSWORD}}
    volumes:
      - mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql
    restart: unless-stopped

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  owncloud_data:
  mysql_data:
templates.securityNotes: Use HTTPS in production
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Login with admin credentials
2. Install apps
3. Configure sharing

templates.name: ArchiveBox
templates.compose:
services:
  archivebox:
    image: archivebox/archivebox:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:8000"
    environment:
      - ALLOWED_HOSTS=*
      - PUBLIC_INDEX=True
      - PUBLIC_SNAPSHOTS=True
      - PUBLIC_ADD_VIEW=False
    volumes:
      - archivebox_data:/data
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  archivebox_data:
templates.securityNotes: Create admin: docker exec archivebox archivebox manage createsuperuser
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Create admin user
2. Add URLs to archive
3. Configure archiving options

templates.name: PicoShare
templates.compose:
services:
  picoshare:
    image: mtlynch/picoshare:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:4001"
    environment:
      - PS_SHARED_SECRET={{SHARED_SECRET}}
    volumes:
      - picoshare_data:/data
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  picoshare_data:
templates.securityNotes: Use shared secret for admin access
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Access with shared secret
2. Upload files
3. Share links

templates.name: Seafile
templates.description: File sync and share with versioning
templates.compose:
services:
  seafile:
    image: seafileltd/seafile-mc:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:80"
    environment:
      - DB_HOST=db
      - DB_ROOT_PASSWD={{DB_ROOT_PASSWORD}}
      - SEAFILE_ADMIN_EMAIL={{ADMIN_EMAIL}}
      - SEAFILE_ADMIN_PASSWORD={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}
      - SEAFILE_SERVER_HOSTNAME={{HOSTNAME}}
    volumes:
      - seafile_data:/shared
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - db
      - memcached

  db:
    image: mariadb:11
    environment:
      - MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD={{DB_ROOT_PASSWORD}}
    volumes:
      - mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql
    restart: unless-stopped

  memcached:
    image: memcached:latest
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  seafile_data:
  mysql_data:
templates.securityNotes: Configure HTTPS for production
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Login with admin credentials
2. Create libraries
3. Install sync clients

templates.name: k0s
templates.compose:
services:
  k0s:
    image: docker.io/k0sproject/k0s:latest
    command: k0s controller --config=/etc/k0s/k0s.yaml
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:6443"
      - "{{KUBE_PORT}}:9443"
    volumes:
      - k0s_data:/var/lib/k0s
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
    privileged: true
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  k0s_data:
templates.securityNotes: Run in privileged mode. Generate kubeconfig for access.
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Get kubeconfig
2. Configure kubectl
3. Deploy workloads

templates.name: Nomad
templates.compose:
services:
  nomad:
    image: hashicorp/nomad:1.7.7
    command: agent -dev -bind=0.0.0.0
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:4646"
      - "{{RPC_PORT}}:4647"
      - "{{SERF_PORT}}:4648"
    environment:
      - NOMAD_SKIP_DOCKER_IMAGE_WARN=true
    volumes:
      - nomad_data:/nomad/data
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
    privileged: true
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  nomad_data:
templates.securityNotes: Dev mode has no ACLs. Configure for production.
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Access Nomad UI
2. Submit jobs
3. Configure ACLs for production

templates.name: Rancher
templates.compose:
services:
  rancher:
    image: rancher/rancher:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:443"
      - "{{HTTP_PORT}}:80"
    volumes:
      - rancher_data:/var/lib/rancher
    privileged: true
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  rancher_data:
templates.securityNotes: Get bootstrap password: docker logs CONTAINER 2>&1 | grep 'Bootstrap Password'
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Get bootstrap password from logs
2. Set admin password
3. Add Kubernetes clusters

templates.name: Changedetection.io
templates.description: Self-hosted website change detection and monitoring with notifications
templates.compose:
services:
  changedetection:
    image: dgtlmoon/changedetection.io:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:5000"
    environment:
      - TZ=UTC
      - BASE_URL=http://localhost:{{PORT}}
    volumes:
      - changedetection_data:/datastore
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  changedetection_data:
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
# Allow External Access to Changedetection.io

## On your VPS

1. Allow the port through firewall:
```bash
sudo ufw allow {{PORT}}/tcp
sudo ufw reload
```

## Access Changedetection.io

Visit: http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}
templates.securityNotes:
- Set a password in the application settings after first login
- Uses file-based storage (no external database)
- For JavaScript-rendered pages, add the Playwright browser sidecar
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Visit the application URL
2. Add your first website URL to monitor
3. Configure notification settings (email, Discord, Slack, etc.)
4. Set check intervals for each watched page

templates.name: Dozzle
templates.description: Real-time Docker container log viewer with search and filtering
templates.compose:
services:
  dozzle:
    image: amir20/dozzle:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:8080"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
    restart: unless-stopped
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
# Allow External Access to Dozzle

## On your VPS

1. Allow the port through firewall:
```bash
sudo ufw allow {{PORT}}/tcp
sudo ufw reload
```

## Access Dozzle

Visit: http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}
templates.securityNotes:
- Docker socket is mounted read-only for security
- Consider adding authentication if exposed to the internet
- Dozzle only reads logs, it cannot modify containers by default
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Visit the application URL
2. View real-time logs from all running Docker containers
3. Use search and filtering to find specific log entries

templates.name: Speedtest Tracker
templates.description: Track and graph internet speed history with automatic scheduled tests
templates.compose:
services:
  speedtest:
    image: ghcr.io/alexjustesen/speedtest-tracker:latest
    ports:
      - "{{PORT}}:80"
      - "{{HTTPS_PORT}}:443"
    environment:
      - PUID=1000
      - PGID=1000
      - APP_KEY={{APP_KEY}}
      - APP_URL=http://localhost:{{PORT}}
      - SPEEDTEST_SCHEDULE=*/30 * * * *
      - APP_TIMEZONE=UTC
      - DB_CONNECTION=sqlite
    volumes:
      - speedtest_config:/config
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  speedtest_config:
templates.externalAccessInstructions:
# Allow External Access to Speedtest Tracker

## On your VPS

1. Allow the port through firewall:
```bash
sudo ufw allow {{PORT}}/tcp
sudo ufw reload
```

## Access Speedtest Tracker

Visit: http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:{{PORT}}
templates.securityNotes:
- Uses embedded SQLite by default
- Speed tests run every 30 minutes by default
- Keep APP_KEY secure
templates.postDeploymentSteps:
1. Visit the application URL
2. Log in with admin@example.com / password
3. Change the default password immediately
4. Configure speed test schedule and notification settings
